title: smart chari ergonamic sensor
rawInput: smart chari ergonamic sensor and processro give alerts if user sits in bad posture for too long.

normalizedData.logic: The posture sensor array continuously captures data representing the user's position. The processing unit analyzes this data, comparing it to pre-defined ergonomic models to classify the current posture. If a non-ergonomic posture is detected, a timer is initiated. If the non-ergonomic posture persists beyond a configurable time threshold, the processing unit transmits a signal to the alert module, which generates a sensory notification to the user.
normalizedData.inputs: Raw sensor data from a plurality of sensors, such as pressure, proximity, or optical sensors, indicating user position and weight distribution.
normalizedData.outputs: A sensory alert, which may be haptic, audible, or visual, to notify the user of prolonged poor posture.
normalizedData.problem: Users often maintain non-ergonomic sitting postures for extended durations, leading to musculoskeletal strain and other health issues, primarily due to a lack of real-time feedback.
normalizedData.abstract: smart chari ergonamic sensor. A system for monitoring and correcting user posture comprises a chair structure, a plurality of sensors disposed within the chair structure to detect a user's sitting position, a processing unit communicatively coupled to the sensors, and an alert module. The processing unit is configured to receive sensor data, analyze the data to determine if the user's posture corresponds to a predefined non-ergonomic state, and activate the alert module if the non-ergonomic state persists for a predetermined period. The alert module provides a sensory notification to prompt the user to adjust their posture. The system is configured to mitigate health risks associated with prolonged improper sitting posture by providing real-time feedback to the user.
normalizedData.variants: The system may be integrated into a new chair, retrofitted onto an existing chair, or embodied as a standalone cushion. Alerts may be transmitted to a companion mobile application via a wireless communication module. The posture analysis algorithm may utilize machine learning to adapt to individual users.
normalizedData.riskFlags: Potential obviousness over prior art combining sensors in furniture with alerts. The definition of 'bad posture' may be subjective and requires clear, objective parameters in the specification to support claim scope and enablement.
normalizedData.bestMethod: A chair with an integrated array of pressure sensors in the seat and backrest is communicatively coupled to a microcontroller. The microcontroller analyzes the pressure map to detect poor posture. An embedded haptic motor provides a discreet vibration alert after a user-configurable time limit of detected poor posture.

components.name: Posture Sensor Array
components.outputs: Raw sensor data (e.g., pressure map, proximity data)
components.figureHint: Sensors embedded in seat and backrest surfaces
components.description: Detects the distribution of a user's weight and position within the chair to generate raw posture data.

components.name: Processing Unit
components.figureHint: Block diagram showing data flow from sensors to alert module
components.description: Receives and analyzes raw sensor data to classify the user's posture and track its duration against a predetermined threshold.

components.name: Alert Module
components.figureHint: Vibration motor or speaker integrated into the chair structure
components.description: Generates a sensory alert, such as a haptic vibration, audible tone, or visual indicator, in response to a signal from the processing unit.

components.name: Power Supply
components.figureHint: Battery pack or AC/DC adapter connection point
components.description: Provides electrical power to the electronic components of the system, such as the sensors, processing unit, and alert module.

normalizedData.objectives: To provide a system that automatically detects a user's sitting posture, determines if the posture is ergonomically incorrect, and generates a timely alert to prompt the user to correct their position.
normalizedData.searchQuery: smart chair ergonomic posture sensor processor alert system for detecting and correcting user sitting position
normalizedData.claimStrategy: Draft a system claim covering the chair, sensors, processor, and alert mechanism. Draft a method claim covering the steps of sensing, processing, determining posture, timing, and alerting. Include dependent claims specifying sensor types (e.g., pressure, optical), alert types (e.g., haptic, audible), and algorithmic details.
normalizedData.drawingsFocus: Figures should clearly illustrate the placement of sensors within the chair structure, a block diagram of the electronic components and their interconnections, and a flowchart detailing the posture detection and alerting logic.
normalizedData.complianceNotes: May be subject to regulations for consumer electronics (e.g., FCC for wireless variants) or as a Class I medical device depending on marketing claims related to health benefits.
normalizedData.recommendedFocus: Emphasize the specific configuration and placement of the sensors, the algorithm used by the processor to distinguish between correct and incorrect posture from the sensor data, and the method of alerting the user.

llmPromptUsed:
You are an expert patent attorney specializing in drafting and structuring patent disclosures across all domains (mechanical, electrical, software, biotech, chemistry, medical devices, materials, aerospace, etc.).

Task: Read the invention description and output ONLY a valid JSON object capturing the key drafting elements.

Rules (must follow strictly):
- Output MUST be a single JSON object, no code fences, no backticks, no prose.
- Use concise, formal patent language suitable for specification drafting.
- Keep each field as a single string (no arrays), except: "components" (array of objects), "cpcCodes" (array of strings), "ipcCodes" (array of strings), and "inventionType" (array of archetype tags).
- Include "inventionType" as the archetype classification (one or more of: MECHANICAL, ELECTRICAL, SOFTWARE, CHEMICAL, BIO, GENERAL). Allow multiple using either an array or a "+"-joined string (e.g., "MECHANICAL+SOFTWARE"); uppercase the values.
- Additionally, provide a compact "searchQuery" string (<= 25 words) optimized for PQAI prior-art search. This should be plain text, ASCII-safe, no quotes, no brackets, no CPC/IPC codes, no labels. Include only essential technical nouns/verbs.
- Use double-quoted keys and strings; avoid line breaks mid-sentence when possible.
 - Keep content succinct; avoid redundancy and marketing language.
 - Components: return up to 8 items maximum by default (more only if essential). Use hierarchy when helpful (module → submodule → sub-submodule). Keep each item's description to one sentence.

TITLE: smart chari ergonamic sensor

INVENTION DESCRIPTION:
smart chari ergonamic sensor and processro give alerts if user sits in bad posture for too long.

Respond in this exact JSON shape:
{
  "searchQuery": "concise plain-text search query (G25 words, ASCII, no quotes/brackets)",
  "problem": "concise statement of the technical problem",
  "objectives": "succinct objectives of the invention",
  "components": [{
    "name": "component name",
    "type": "MAIN_CONTROLLER|SUBSYSTEM|MODULE|INTERFACE|SENSOR|ACTUATOR|PROCESSOR|MEMORY|DISPLAY|COMMUNICATION|POWER_SUPPLY|OTHER",
    "description": "technical role in the system",
    "inputs": "optional: key inputs/signals/data",
    "outputs": "optional: key outputs/actions/data",
    "dependencies": "optional: other components relied on",
    "figureHint": "optional: what to highlight in figures",
    "parent": "optional: parent component name if this is a submodule",
    "level": "optional: 0 for root modules, 1 for child, 2 for grandchild, etc.",
    "sequence": "optional: order within its level (1-based)",
    "numberingHint": "optional: preferred hundreds bucket e.g., 100|200|300|400|500|600|700|800|900"
  }],
  "inventionType": ["MECHANICAL", "SOFTWARE"],
  "logic": "how components interact to achieve the objectives",
  "inputs": "key inputs/signals/data required",
  "outputs": "key outputs/actions/data produced",
  "variants": "notable embodiments or alternatives",
  "bestMethod": "preferred implementation at filing date",
  "fieldOfRelevance": "primary domain (e.g., Mechanical, Electrical, Software, Medical Device, Biotech, Chemistry, Materials, Aerospace)",
  "subfield": "more specific area (e.g., fluid mechanics, image processing, polymer chemistry)",
  "recommendedFocus": "what to emphasize in drafting for this field",
  "complianceNotes": "regulatory or standards-related notes if relevant",
  "drawingsFocus": "what figures should emphasize given the field",
  "claimStrategy": "high-level claim drafting approach suited to this field",
  "riskFlags": "any potential enablement or patentability risks to watch",
  "abstract": "G150 words abstract that begins exactly with the title; neutral tone; no claims/advantages/numerals",
  "cpcCodes": ["primary CPC code like H04L 29/08", "optional secondary"],
  "ipcCodes": ["primary IPC code like G06F 17/30", "optional secondary"]
}
